audit_status.source_base: Greek Acts text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for Timothy, Spirit travel direction, Macedonian vision, Lydia, enslaved divination girl, Philippi imprisonment, earthquake, jailer household, citizen-rights release.

verses.restored: He came also to Derbe and to Lystra. Look, a certain disciple was there named Timothy, son of a trusting Judean woman and a Greek father.
He was well witnessed by the siblings in Lystra and Iconium.
Paul wanted this one to go out with him; taking him, he circumcised him because of the Judeans in those places, for they all knew his father was Greek.
As they were passing through the cities, they were delivering to them to guard the decrees judged by the apostles and elders in Jerusalem.
Therefore the assemblies were being strengthened in the trust and were increasing in number day by day.
They went through the Phrygian and Galatian region, having been forbidden by the holy Spirit to speak the word in Asia.
Coming near Mysia, they were attempting to go into Bithynia, and the Spirit of Jesus did not permit them.
Passing by Mysia, they came down to Troas.
A vision appeared to Paul during the night: a certain Macedonian man was standing and urging him, saying, Come over into Macedonia and help us.
When he saw the vision, immediately we sought to go out into Macedonia, concluding that the Divine had called us to announce good news to them.
Putting out to sea from Troas, we ran straight to Samothrace, and the next day to Neapolis,
and from there to Philippi, which is a leading city of that district of Macedonia, a Roman colony. We were remaining in this city some days.
On the Sabbath day, we went outside the gate by a river where we supposed there was a place of prayer, and sitting down, we were speaking to the women who had gathered.
A certain woman named Lydia, a seller of purple cloth from the city of Thyatira, revering the Divine, was hearing. The Lord opened her heart to pay attention to the things spoken by Paul.
When she and her household were baptized, she urged us, saying, If you have judged me faithful to the Lord, come into my house and remain. And she prevailed upon us.
It happened as we were going to prayer, a certain enslaved girl having a spirit of python/divination met us, who provided much profit to her masters by fortune-telling.
This one, following Paul and us, cried out, saying, These humans are servants of the Most High Divine, who announce to you a way of salvation/healing.
She was doing this for many days. Paul, being greatly troubled, turned and said to the spirit, I command you in the name of Jesus Messiah to come out from her. And it came out that very hour.
Her masters, seeing that the hope of their profit had gone out, seized Paul and Silas and dragged them into the marketplace before the rulers.
Bringing them to the magistrates, they said, These humans, being Judeans, are disturbing our city,
and are proclaiming customs that are not lawful for us to receive or practice, being Romans.
The crowd rose together against them, and the magistrates, tearing off their garments, commanded them to be beaten with rods.
Having laid many blows on them, they threw them into prison, commanding the jailer to guard them securely.
Having received such a command, he threw them into the inner prison and secured their feet in the stocks.
About midnight, Paul and Silas, praying, were singing hymns to the Divine, and the prisoners were listening to them.
Suddenly a great earthquake happened, so that the foundations of the prison were shaken. Immediately all the doors were opened, and everyone's bonds were loosened.
The jailer, becoming awake and seeing the prison doors opened, drew the sword and was about to kill himself, supposing the prisoners had escaped.
But Paul cried with a great voice, saying, Do nothing harmful to yourself, for we are all here.
Calling for lights, he rushed in, and trembling, fell before Paul and Silas.
Bringing them outside, he said, Lords, what must I do to be saved/healed?
They said, Trust upon the Lord Jesus, and you will be saved/healed, you and your house.
They spoke the word of the Lord to him, together with all those in his house.
Taking them in that hour of the night, he washed their wounds, and he was baptized at once, he and all his household.
Bringing them up into the house, he set a table before them, and rejoiced with his whole household, having trusted the Divine.
When day came, the magistrates sent the officers, saying, Release those humans.
The jailer reported these words to Paul: The magistrates have sent to release you. Now therefore go out and travel in peace.

But Paul said to them, Having beaten us publicly without trial, men who are Romans, they threw us into prison; and now do they cast us out secretly? No indeed. Let them come themselves and bring us out.
The officers reported these words to the magistrates, and they were afraid when they heard that they were Romans.
Coming, they appealed to them, and bringing them out, asked them to leave the city.
Going out from the prison, they entered Lydia's house; and seeing the siblings, they encouraged them and departed.
